page: Microsoft Azure D16nlds_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine belonging to the Dnldv6 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage requirements, the instance includes a bundled 945 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage configurations. This local storage capacity provides fast scratch space, though users must manage data persistence as local disks are typically ephemeral. The instance does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server as a compute-heavy option where memory footprint is minimized to optimize resource costs. This hardware profile is designed for network-intensive workloads, web serving, and distributed compute tasks that do not require massive memory allocations. It is also suitable for database replicas or application servers that leverage local storage for caching while relying on external databases for persistent storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D16nlds_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnldv6 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core resource ratio. The server includes 945 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This low-memory, compute-aligned configuration is designed to optimize resource density for network-intensive operations, web serving, and distributed computing tasks where memory requirements are modest relative to processing power.
